m_staging_strategy: GDC pathologic M preferred; fall back to clinical M if pathologic is MX/missing. MX excluded from analysis.

diagnostics.top2a_direction: high TOP2A -> more M1 (SAME direction as TCGA-KIRC)
diagnostics.epas1_direction: high EPAS1 -> less M1 (SAME direction as TCGA-KIRC, HIF-2alpha well-differentiated biology)

failure_analysis.ci_lower_fail_reason: At n_m1=20, observed AUROC=0.683 yields wide bootstrap CI [0.542, 0.830]. Simulation shows n_m1~81 needed for AUROC 0.726 to clear ci_lower > 0.60. This is a statistical power limitation (underpowered cohort), not signal absence.
failure_analysis.delta_baseline_fail_reason: TOP2A alone AUROC=0.691 > compound AUROC=0.683. EPAS1 sign-inv AUROC=0.572 (weak) adds sampling noise at small n, slightly reducing compound performance vs TOP2A alone. At n_m1=81 (TCGA scale), EPAS1 provides consistent +0.069 lift; at n_m1=20, the same feature adds more noise than signal.
failure_analysis.key_finding: DIRECTION of both TOP2A and EPAS1 is preserved on CPTAC-3 (consistent with TCGA-KIRC). The compound law is directionally consistent but statistically underpowered: AUROC 0.683 vs 0.726 on TCGA-KIRC, perm_p=0.0060, but n_m1=20 is insufficient for ci_lower > 0.60 at this AUROC level.

m_staging_notes.note: 106/261 kidney cases excluded due to MX/missing M-staging. 15 of 20 M1 cases determined by clinical (imaging-only) staging. Stage IV cross-check: 25 Stage IV patients; 20 confirmed M1, 3 annotated M0 (may include T4N0M0), 2 missing M-stage.

power_simulation.conclusion: Simulation confirms: at n_m1=20, true AUROC ~0.726 yields ci_lower ~0.59 (below 0.60 threshold). Need n_m1>=40 for reliable ci_lower > 0.60 at this effect size. CPTAC-3 M1 prevalence in matched cohort: ~13% (20/155).
